title: Conjecture 2
statement_text: For every hereditary class $C$ of structures, FO model checking is FPT on $C$ if and only if $C$ is monadically dependent.
context_text: This conjecture (cited as [28, Conjecture 8.2]) underpins the paper's notion of delineation: a class $C$ is delineated by twin-width if, for every hereditary closure $D$ of a subclass of $C$, $D$ has bounded twin-width if and only if $D$ is monadically dependent, which would confirm Conjecture 2 within $C$. The paper proves effective delineation for interval graphs and rooted directed path graphs, thereby settling Conjecture 2 for all hereditary subclasses of those classes.
paper_contribution: Proves Conjecture 2 for all hereditary subclasses of interval graphs and rooted directed path graphs by establishing that these classes are effectively delineated by twin-width; also shows Conjecture 2 cannot be settled this way for directed path graphs (multiple roots), axis-parallel two-lengthed segment graphs, and visibility graphs of simple polygons.
notes: Attributed to [28, Conjecture 8.2] in the paper; the exact authors of reference [28] cannot be determined from the provided text extract. The paper says 'see for instance', suggesting this is a known community conjecture with multiple attributions.

title: Open Question (unit segment graphs delineation)
statement_text: Are unit segment graphs delineated (by twin-width)?
context_text: The paper establishes that axis-parallel $H_t$-free unit segment graphs have bounded twin-width, while axis-parallel $H_4$-free two-lengthed segment graphs have unbounded twin-width, and that (triangle-free) pure axis-parallel unit segment graphs have unbounded twin-width. This places unit segment graphs at the frontier between delineated and non-delineated intersection graph classes, and the authors explicitly leave their delineation status as an open question.
notes: Stated verbatim in the abstract: 'We leave as an open question whether unit segment graphs are delineated.'
